title: ci: audit stale.yml behavior on VS14's tiny issue set
description:
## Context
`.github/workflows/stale.yml` runs daily (`0 0 * * *`). Latest run:
success. Unlike the other scheduled workflows, this one is doing
something — it uses `actions/stale@v9` to mark untriaged issues/PRs
stale after 90 days of inactivity.

```yaml
days-before-stale: 90
remove-stale-when-updated: true
only-issue-labels: 'S: Untriaged'
stale-issue-label: 'Stale'
days-before-issue-close: -1      ← never auto-close
```

## Why this needs assessment (not urgent but worth a look)
1. **It acts on our issues / PRs with real consequences**. Unlike
   the others, a misconfiguration here would spam stale labels on
   random tickets.
2. **VS14 has ~0 issues open right now**, so its impact is currently
   nil — but the moment we have any bug reports, they're subject to
   this automation.
3. **Upstream's label vocabulary** (`S: Untriaged`, `Stale`) was
   imported from Delta-V/SS14. If we haven't created those labels,
   the workflow quietly no-ops. Could be fine, or could silently
   mask that we should be triaging things.
4. **`days-before-issue-close: -1` = never close** is a safe
   default, but we should confirm we want that behavior (vs the
   more common "warn at 60, close at 90" pattern).

## Task
1. Check which labels currently exist in the repo
   (`gh label list --repo azigler/vacation-station-14`).
2. Create `S: Untriaged` and `Stale` if missing; otherwise the
   workflow is just a no-op every night.
3. Decide: keep "warn only, never close" (current), or adopt
   "close after N days" semantics.
4. Document the chosen policy in `CONTRIBUTING.md` or similar so
   people understand what triggers the Stale label.

## Options
### Option A: keep as-is + create labels
Current behavior is safe and matches SS14 convention. Just make
sure the labels exist so it's not a silent no-op.

### Option B: tune the thresholds
30 / 60 / 90 days depending on what fits the project cadence.

### Option C: disable
If we don't want any automation acting on our issue tracker.

## Recommendation
**A**. Create the labels (5-minute task), leave the behavior
alone, document it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `S: Untriaged` and `Stale` labels exist in the repo
- [ ] Decision recorded on thresholds / close semantics
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md has a Triage Policy note
- [ ] Next scheduled run completes without action-required warnings

title: impl: adapt SS14 grafana dashboards for VS14
description:
## Context
SS14 community provides two Grafana dashboards as JSON exports:
- Game Servers (uid K0LTdKmMk): fleet overview
- Perf Metrics (uid qyFqpFiGk): per-server deep dive with Logs panel

Source: docs.spacestation14.com/en/community/infrastructure-reference/grafana-dashboards.html

## Dependencies
- vs-2p3 (prometheus/loki/grafana stack) must be running

## Task
Adapt the upstream SS14 dashboards for Vacation Station. Store JSON in repo.
Provision automatically on Grafana startup via provisioning.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Download and adapt
- [ ] Fetch raw dashboard JSON from docs page
- [ ] Store at ops/grafana/dashboards/game-servers.json
- [ ] Store at ops/grafana/dashboards/perf-metrics.json

### Adaptations
- [ ] Replace datasource UIDs with our Grafana's
- [ ] Verify `job="gameservers"` matches our Prometheus scrape config
- [ ] Verify `server` label values match (our instance name)
- [ ] Rename where appropriate (e.g. "Wizden Servers" -> "Vacation Station")

### Provisioning
- [ ] ops/grafana/provisioning/datasources/datasources.yml
- [ ] ops/grafana/provisioning/dashboards/dashboards.yml with file provider
  pointing at /var/lib/grafana/dashboards/
- [ ] docker-compose mounts dashboards + provisioning into Grafana container
- [ ] On fresh Grafana startup, both dashboards auto-appear

### Verification
- [ ] Game Servers dashboard shows our instance
- [ ] Perf Metrics dashboard's $Server variable populated with our instance name
- [ ] Logs panel on Perf Metrics shows live Loki logs
- [ ] Connections panel works (requires Postgres datasource configured)

### Documentation
- [ ] docs/OPERATIONS.md dashboard section
- [ ] Customization notes (where to edit for fork-specific metrics)
- [ ] How to add new panels or dashboards

title: human: advertise vacation-station on ss14 public hub
description:
## Context
Vacation Station 14 runtime config disables hub advertisement by default.
Players can still connect via direct URL (`ss14://51.81.33.136:1212`), but
the server won't appear in any launcher's public browser. Flip only when
ready for cold inbound traffic.

## Pre-flip checklist
- [ ] Run is stable: server has survived multiple full rounds without
      watchdog restart / crash-loop.
- [ ] Admin toolkit: at least one admin account seeded in postgres; admin
      commands (kick/ban/announce) tested from the client.
- [ ] **Rules & MOTD written** (see vs-rules bead) — hub launch with blank
      or upstream-default rules is a bad first impression.
- [ ] Auth decision made: flip to `mode = 1` (Required) before advertising
      unless you're consciously running a guest-friendly server. Guests
      via hub = grief farm in practice.
- [ ] vs-2y8 done (watchdog behind Caddy+HTTPS) — hub moderators and
      curious players will probe `:5000` and see plain HTTP.
- [ ] Discord crash alerts: `Notification.DiscordWebhook` populated in
      `/opt/ss14-watchdog/appsettings.yml` so we know when the server
      falls over.
- [ ] Capacity: enough resource headroom for first-wave hub traffic
      (recent metrics: tick rate, entity count, RSS under load). Cold
      starts from the hub can be 30+ players within minutes.

## Task — when ready
### Decide the hub list
Default SS14 hub: `hub.spacestation14.io` (Wizards Den). Adding additional
hubs is a comma-separated value in `hub.hub_urls`. Candidate hubs:

1. **Wizards Den** — `https://hub.spacestation14.io/` (primary, always include)
2. **Delta-V** — if Delta-V runs a distinct community hub, include it. VERIFY
   before listing. As of fork time we didn't confirm. Delta-V's server list
   lives somewhere public — check deltavstation.com or their Discord for
   the hub URL. If Delta-V uses Wizden hub only, skip.
3. **Starlight / other downstream communities** — some fork communities
   cross-list. Research before committing.
4. **Our own hub** — medium-term; we may eventually stand up a VS14-flavored
   hub that lists us plus sibling forks. Separate bead.

Multi-hub is free (each POST is independent) and discoverability-positive.
Being listed only on one hub means your server is invisible to clients
using other launchers configured against different hubs.

### Execute
1. Edit `/opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/config.toml`, find
   `[hub]` section.
2. Flip `advertise = true` (check the exact cvar name against the current
   template — might be `hub.advertise` or just `advertise` within the
   section).
3. Set `hub_urls` to the comma-separated list of hubs chosen above.
4. Confirm `server_url` is correct (the URL clients connect to after
   clicking through the browser). For now: `ss14://51.81.33.136:1212`.
   Once you have a domain, flip to `ss14://vacation.yourdomain.com:1212`.
5. Optionally set `tags` — e.g. `18+ rp-lite medium-pop hard-fork` — for
   launcher-side filtering.
6. Trigger server restart:
   ```
   curl -sS -u "vacation-station:$API_TOKEN" -X POST \
     https://<watchdog-url>/instances/vacation-station/restart
   ```
7. Verify:
   - Public SS14 launcher → server browser → "Vacation Station" appears
     within ~60s on each hub you listed.
   - Direct connect still works.
   - No hub-related errors in `journalctl -u ss14-watchdog --since '2 min ago'`.
   - Each hub's server list shows you at the expected tier (new, low-pop
     = sorted near bottom; that's normal).

### First-hour watch
Hub-new-server discovery wave can be 20-50 concurrent connections in the
first hour. Watch Grafana's Perf Metrics dashboard for tick rate drops,
entity count spikes, memory pressure. Be ready to flip Advertise back off
and POST /restart if the server's unstable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `hub.advertise = true` on the live host
- [ ] `hub.hub_urls` populated with every hub we want to list on
- [ ] Server visible in the SS14 launcher's public browser on each hub
      within ~5 minutes
- [ ] `server.rules` / `server.motd` populated and sensible
- [ ] At least one active admin account defined in postgres
- [ ] First-hour watch complete; no restart storm, no OOM, tick rate stable
- [ ] Record flip timestamp + launch-day traffic notes in this bead

## Delisting risk — read before flipping
The Wizden hub has a moderation team that can DELIST your server for:
- Inadequate rules / MOTD
- Obvious grief / no-RP angle
- Harassment or discriminatory content in server identity
- Impersonating another server
- Security issues that affect connecting clients (crashes, RCE)

Usually you'll get a warning first. Repeat offenses = removed without
notice. Build good ops habits now so you don't get caught out later.

## Notes
- Advertising is one-way-ish: you can flip it back off, but bookmarks
  persist. Changing the public IP / server_url is the only way to "hide"
  once out.
- Being on multiple hubs doesn't increase your grief risk — the SAME
  players see you via different launchers. It just reaches more people.

title: ops: assess + selectively fork ss14 ancillary services (cookbook, map-server, etc.)
description:
## Context
The SS14 ecosystem has several ancillary services beyond the game engine
itself: map renderer, map server, cookbook / crafting lookup, docs site,
hub software, admin webapp, launcher, etc. Some already exist in this
repo; some are separate projects we could fork + integrate into our
monorepo; some we don't need at all.

User's ask: assess each candidate for "hard-fork into our monorepo and
cherry-pick upstream changes" vs "vanilla (run upstream or rely on
upstream-hosted)" vs "don't bother at all."

## Candidate repos to assess
**REQUIRES VERIFICATION of exact upstream repo URLs — the
orchestrator guessed at names; confirm before committing to any fork.**

### Already in-tree
1. **Content.MapRenderer** — in `/opt/vacation-station/Content.MapRenderer/`.
   Build workflow: `.github/workflows/build-map-renderer.yml`. Emits
   map PNGs for a given prototype. No action.

### External — candidates for fork-into-monorepo
2. **Cookbook / crafting lookup** — Unknown exact repo. If it's the
   community-maintained player-facing recipe browser, VS14's recipes
   diverge from upstream the moment we add a single new item, so fork
   is natural. Hosting a `cookbook.vacation.yourdomain.com` that shows
   OUR recipes (not upstream's) is real player value.
3. **Map server** — If by "map server" you mean the service that
   renders + hosts map previews for launcher / website, it could pair
   with Content.MapRenderer's output. Fork-in-monorepo potentially
   worthwhile.

### External — probably vanilla
4. **Documentation site** (docs.spacestation14.com) — Upstream docs
   describe SS14 mechanics. VS14 inherits ~99% of mechanics. Forking
   means maintaining a mostly-identical copy. Vanilla (link out to
   upstream docs) is cheaper. If/when we diverge substantially, add
   a VS14-specific docs site alongside.
5. **SS14.Admin** (admin webapp) — Upstream's admin UI wraps the game
   server's admin API. Works out of the box if we point it at our
   postgres + game server. Vanilla seems fine.
6. **SS14.Hub** — The hub software upstream runs. We advertise TO it
   (see vs-17n). Only worth forking if we want to run our OWN hub
   (listing VS14 + sibling forks). Long-term aspiration, not now.
7. **SS14.Launcher** — The client launcher. Too much surface area to
   fork for a single-fork community. Let players use the upstream
   launcher.

### External — unknown, verify first
8. **"Document sim"** — User mentioned this but the orchestrator
   couldn't identify the exact repo. Verify what you meant —
   possibilities:
   - docs.spacestation14.com (the docfx site)
   - An in-game documentation generator
   - A community fan project
   Resolve this reference before scoping.

## Fork-or-vanilla decision matrix
For each candidate, score on:
- **Diverges from upstream?** (our content makes the upstream version
  wrong for VS14) → push toward fork
- **High traffic / critical path?** (players expect it to always work
  and match our content) → push toward fork
- **Upstream churn rate?** (high churn = expensive fork) → push toward
  vanilla
- **Customization need?** (branding / integrations with our stuff) →
  push toward fork
- **Code size?** (small = cheap to fork, large = expensive) → small
  pushes toward fork
- **Replaces something we lack?** (no VS14-equivalent exists) → push
  toward fork (there's no "vanilla" to fall back to)

## Task
1. **Enumerate** the actual upstream repos for each candidate. Paste URLs.
2. **Score** each against the matrix above in a table in this bead.
3. **Decide** per candidate: fork / vanilla / skip.
4. **For each "fork" decision**:
   - Create a landing directory in monorepo (`services/<name>/` or
     `web/apps/<name>/`)
   - Initial commit = unmodified upstream import, preserving their git
     history via `git subtree add --prefix=services/<name> <upstream-repo>
     main --squash` (or no squash if you want their history)
   - Add a README stating upstream source + divergence-tracking commit
   - Add to `docs/upstream-sync.md` — which upstream remotes to track
5. **Expand `/upstream-sync` skill** to include pulling from these
   additional upstream remotes, not just Delta-V.

## Structure after this bead lands
```
vacation-station-14/
├── Content.{Server,Client,Shared}/
├── web/                            (vs-2dr)
├── services/
│   ├── discord-bot/                (vs-2l2)
│   ├── cookbook/                   ← forked, IF decision = fork
│   └── map-server/                 ← forked, IF decision = fork
├── ops/
└── docs/
    └── upstream-sync.md            ← new: lists all upstream remotes
```

Each forked service has its own systemd unit / docker-compose /
Caddyfile block. Consistent with how ops/observability/ and
ops/watchdog/ are structured.

## Dependencies
- **vs-2dr** (website) — some services integrate AS website routes
  (cookbook under /cookbook), others as separate subdomains
- **vs-2y8** (Caddy) — each service gets a subdomain in the reverse
  proxy

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each candidate's upstream repo URL confirmed + linked
- [ ] Fork/vanilla/skip decision recorded per candidate with reasoning
- [ ] For each "fork" decision: directory created, initial import commit,
      README with upstream link
- [ ] `docs/upstream-sync.md` lists all upstream remotes
- [ ] `/upstream-sync` skill updated to cover the new remotes
- [ ] Caddyfile blocks + systemd units for each forked service stubbed
      (actual config deferred to per-service beads)

## Out of scope
- Actually building / customizing the forked services — this bead is
  the decision + import scaffolding. Per-service customization gets
  its own bead once the import lands.
- Cherry-pick automation (later, once divergence becomes painful)

title: ops: add services-flake dev stack (postgres + observability)
description:
## Context
Production bring-up of postgres + prometheus + loki + grafana is handled by the ops scripts + docker-compose we shipped (vs-3ty/vs-h3u/vs-2p3). That path is correct for the Ubuntu host. For local dev we want a zero-sudo, ephemeral equivalent so contributors can validate config changes without touching docker or a real host.

services-flake (https://community.flake.parts/services-flake) is a flake-parts module set that wraps process-compose to run a stack of services as user processes with data under a project-local dir.

Goal: `nix run .#dev-services` boots postgres + prometheus + loki + grafana on localhost against the SAME configs we committed in `ops/`, with data in `.data/` (gitignored).

## Dependencies
- vs-2ti (ops validation tools in dev shell) — not strictly blocking, but land that first so `promtool` etc. are available to validate configs the dev stack loads.

## Task
Extend `flake.nix` to add a services-flake output that composes:
- `services.postgres.pg1` — port 5432, database `vacation_station`, user `vs14`, password `dev-only-insecure` (literal — this is dev-only, document that)
- `services.prometheus.prom1` — config from `ops/observability/prometheus.yml` (or a dev-overlay that points the scrape target at the dev SS14 server port on localhost)
- `services.loki.loki1` — config from `ops/observability/loki-config.yml` (or dev-overlay as needed)
- `services.grafana.graf1` — admin password `admin` (dev only), provisioning dir pointing at `ops/observability/grafana/provisioning`, dashboards dir at `ops/observability/grafana/dashboards`

## Scope
1. **`flake.nix` inputs**: add `services-flake` (from `github:juspay/services-flake`) and `process-compose-flake` (`github:platonic-systems/process-compose-flake`).
2. **`flake.nix` outputs**: switch from `flake-utils.lib.eachDefaultSystem` to `flake-parts.lib.mkFlake` (services-flake requires flake-parts). Preserve the existing `devShells.default = import ./shell.nix { inherit pkgs; }` output — don't break the dev shell that's already working.
3. **New flake output**: `packages.<system>.dev-services` (or `apps.<system>.dev-services`) — the process-compose entrypoint.
4. **Service configs**:
   - Postgres: `initialDatabases = [{ name = "vacation_station"; }]` to match prod schema
   - Prometheus: load our committed `ops/observability/prometheus.yml`. If the `scrape_configs` target `host.docker.internal:44880` needs a dev override (docker-specific hostname won't resolve in dev), put a small nix-side overlay that rewrites the target to `localhost:44880`. Document this.
   - Loki: load `ops/observability/loki-config.yml`. If the `path_prefix` needs to be rewritten to a dev-local dir, do it in the nix expression.
   - Grafana: provisioning + dashboards from the committed paths. Datasource URLs point at the dev-stack services.
5. **Data persistence**: all services write state to `.data/{postgres,prometheus,loki,grafana}/` (project-relative). Update root `.gitignore` to ignore `.data/`.
6. **Docs**: add a "Dev Stack" subsection to `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md` (create if absent) covering:
   - `nix run .#dev-services` to start
   - How to view logs (process-compose TUI)
   - How to reset (`rm -rf .data/`)
   - Credentials (dev-only literals; never reuse for prod)
   - Relationship to production (same configs, same schema, different storage)
   - SS14 connection: point game server's `config.toml` `[database]` at `localhost:5432` user `vs14` pw `dev-only-insecure`; `[metrics]` and `[loki]` already point at localhost.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `nix flake check` passes (or at minimum: `nix-instantiate --parse flake.nix` succeeds and `nix flake show` lists the new output)
- [ ] `nix run .#dev-services --help` (or equivalent) resolves — you don't need to actually boot the stack in the worktree, just prove the expression evaluates
- [ ] Existing `devShells.default` still evaluates (preserve `nix develop` behavior)
- [ ] `.data/` gitignored; no committed state files
- [ ] Dev-only credentials clearly commented as such in flake.nix and docs
- [ ] docs/DEVELOPMENT.md section documents bring-up, reset, credentials, schema-parity claim, and point back to vs-3ty/vs-2p3 for prod
- [ ] Single commit, `Bead: vs-1bb` trailer, gitmoji `:sparkles:`

## Out of scope
- Do NOT modify the production setup scripts or docker-compose
- Do NOT add SS14.Watchdog as a services-flake service (it's a single supervisor binary, not a service we'd want in dev alongside the stack — contributors run SS14 directly via `dotnet run` in dev)
- Do NOT attempt to actually boot the stack inside the worktree (process-compose wants a TTY and the services want ports; just prove the expression evaluates)

## Environment note
Prepend `eval "$(direnv export bash)" 2>/dev/null;` to nix commands so the flake toolchain is active. See CLAUDE.md "Nix env in worktree subagents".

## References to consult
- services-flake quickstart: https://community.flake.parts/services-flake
- Available services: https://community.flake.parts/services-flake/services
- flake-parts: https://flake.parts

## Commit
Example message:
```
:sparkles: ops: nix services-flake dev stack for postgres + observability

- flake.nix: add services-flake + process-compose-flake inputs, migrate to flake-parts
- packages.<system>.dev-services: postgres + prometheus + loki + grafana,
  data in ./.data/ (gitignored), binds to localhost, zero sudo required
- Services read the same configs committed in ops/ (with small dev overlays
  for host.docker.internal -> localhost and data paths)
- docs/DEVELOPMENT.md: Dev Stack section (bring-up, reset, credentials,
  schema parity with prod, how to wire a dev SS14 instance against it)
- devShells.default preserved unchanged
- .gitignore: .data/

Dev-only credentials embedded (documented as such). Production path is
unchanged (vs-3ty postgres + vs-2p3 docker-compose stack remain canonical).

Bead: vs-1bb
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

title: skill: /services — dev (nix services-flake) vs prod (systemd+docker) handbook
description:
## Context
We now have TWO parallel stack realities, and the lines are blurring in
conversations:

1. **Dev (nix services-flake)**: `nix run .#dev-services` boots
   postgres / prometheus / loki / grafana locally with state in
   `./.data/` (gitignored). Zero sudo. Insecure dev-only credentials
   literal in flake.nix. Data ephemeral by design — `rm -rf .data/` to
   reset.

2. **Prod (systemd + docker-compose)**: live on zig-computer right now.
   Postgres via apt + systemd. Watchdog / SS14.Server under
   ss14-watchdog.service. Observability via docker-compose in
   ops/observability/. Real credentials, persistent data in
   /var/lib/, /opt/, docker volumes. Reset = destructive surgery.

Each stack has conventions for bring-up, inspection, reset, and debugging
that are NOT interchangeable. Running dev commands against prod is
catastrophic; running prod commands against dev is usually pointless.
The existing `/nix` skill covers dev; prod is spread across docs/OPERATIONS.md
and tribal memory. Need a handbook.

## Task
Create `.claude/skills/services/SKILL.md`. Contents:

### 1. The two stacks — side-by-side table
Mirror what exists where: each service's dev location + prod location.
Example shape:

| Service | Dev | Prod |
|---|---|---|
| Postgres | services-flake `postgres.pg1`, `.data/postgres/` | apt `postgresql-17`, `/var/lib/postgresql/17/main/`, systemd |
| Prometheus | services-flake `prometheus.prom1` | docker-compose `prom/prometheus:v2.54.1` |
| Loki | services-flake `loki.loki1` | docker-compose `grafana/loki:3.2.1` |
| Grafana | services-flake `grafana.graf1` (:3200) | docker-compose `grafana/grafana:11.3.0` (:3200) |
| Watchdog | N/A (don't run in dev) | systemd `ss14-watchdog.service` |
| SS14 server | `dotnet run` directly | child of watchdog |

### 2. When to use which (decision tree)
| Goal | Stack | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Validate a `prometheus.yml` change | dev | ephemeral, fast reset, can't break prod |
| Test a DB migration | dev | same schema, guest-ignorable creds |
| Investigate a live bug | prod logs | dev reproduction rarely matches real traffic |
| Write a Grafana dashboard | dev first, copy to prod | dev is your scratchpad |
| Reset everything | see reset procedures |   |
| Test Discord webhook config | prod with a scratch channel | real webhook mechanics |

### 3. Bring-up procedures
**Dev** (full sequence):
```bash
eval "$(direnv export bash)"  # if not already
rm -rf .data                  # fresh state, optional
nix run .#dev-services
```

**Prod** (commands, not "walk-throughs" — the actual invocations you
repeat when something's been stopped):
```bash
sudo systemctl start postgresql ss14-watchdog ss14-backup.timer
cd /opt/vacation-station/ops/observability && docker compose up -d
```

### 4. Reset procedures
**Dev nuclear** (cheap, do whenever):
```bash
pkill -f process-compose
rm -rf .data
```

**Prod nuclear** (expensive, only when you've decided):
```bash
sudo systemctl stop ss14-watchdog
docker compose -f /opt/vacation-station/ops/observability/docker-compose.yml down
sudo -u postgres dropdb vacation_station
sudo -u postgres createdb -O vs14 vacation_station
sudo rm -rf /opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/data/*
sudo rm -rf /var/backups/vacation-station/*   # ← consider archiving first!
docker volume rm vs14-observability_{prometheus,loki,grafana}-data
# then full bring-up per section 3
```

Also include **surgical resets** for individual services (just reset
Prometheus TSDB, just drop the DB, etc.).

### 5. Inspection (commands that are different between dev + prod)
- Process tree: dev = `process-compose attach`; prod = `systemctl status`,
  `docker compose ps`
- Logs: dev = process-compose TUI; prod = `journalctl --user -u X` /
  `docker compose logs X`
- Config reload: dev = Ctrl+C + re-run; prod = systemctl restart X /
  docker compose restart X (doesn't re-evaluate YAML unless recreated)
- Port checks: `ss -tln` works for both; the ports are (mostly) identical
  by design

### 6. Secrets handling — different rules
- Dev: literal in flake.nix, documented as dev-only
- Prod: generated on first install (postgres password, watchdog
  ApiToken, grafana admin). Captured in password manager once. Live
  only in `/etc/vacation-station/*.env` (gitignored) or inline in
  `/opt/.../config.toml`, `/opt/.../appsettings.yml` — NEVER in the
  repo

### 7. Gotchas we've hit
- Dev postgres data `/opt/vacation-station/.data/postgres` vs prod
  postgres data `/var/lib/postgresql/17/main` — utterly different;
  don't let scripts confuse them
- Dev Grafana at :3200, prod Grafana at :3200 — SAME PORT → they
  CAN'T run simultaneously on the same host. This is a feature.
- Config file paths: repo-committed templates end in `.example`;
  populated runtime versions are in `/opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/...`
  or `ops/observability/secrets/...`. Don't edit the `.example`
  expecting changes to take effect; it only propagates on fresh
  deploy.

### 8. Quick reference card
One-screen summary of the most-used commands, organized by verb (start,
stop, reset, log, exec into shell, check status).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `.claude/skills/services/SKILL.md` exists, follows the skill
      format (frontmatter with name/description/type)
- [ ] All 8 sections above are populated with real commands (copy-pasted
      from the actual host)
- [ ] Dev nuclear reset tested by running it; resulting fresh boot
      verified
- [ ] Prod nuclear reset procedures reviewed by a second person before
      ever executing (NEVER run this bead's prod reset as a test — it's
      destructive)
- [ ] Cross-link from `/nix` skill + `docs/OPERATIONS.md` pointing to
      this skill

## Out of scope
- Ops SLOs / incident runbooks — separate skill (once we have real
  production incidents to draw from)
- Cost monitoring (you'd want this once the VPS bill becomes a factor)

## Notes
- This skill should feel like an operator's cheat sheet, not a
  walkthrough. Assume the reader already understands the concepts;
  they need the commands fast.

title: human: run vs-h3u watchdog setup on host
description:
## Context
vs-h3u shipped setup.watchdog.sh, systemd unit, appsettings.yml.example, instance-bootstrap.sh, and extended config.toml.example + docs/OPERATIONS.md. None of it has run on the host.

## Dependencies
- vs-y6o (postgres host setup) complete — watchdog unit has `After=postgresql.service`

## Task — run on the target host
1. `sudo ./setup.watchdog.sh` (optionally `WATCHDOG_REF=<tag-or-sha> sudo -E ./setup.watchdog.sh` to pin upstream).
   - Creates system user `ss14:ss14`
   - Publishes SS14.Watchdog to `/opt/ss14-watchdog/`
   - Installs `ss14-watchdog.service`, enables (not starts)
   - Generates ApiToken into a fresh `appsettings.yml` (only if absent)
2. Capture the generated ApiToken from `/opt/ss14-watchdog/appsettings.yml`. Store in password manager under "Vacation Station / watchdog / vacation-station ApiToken". Never commit.
3. `sudo ./ops/watchdog/instance-bootstrap.sh` — sets up `/opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/` layout and seeds `config.toml` from the template.
4. Edit `/opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/config.toml`: fill in postgres password from password manager; adjust hub/auth/status tags as desired.
5. Drop a Robust.Server build into `/opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/binaries/` (Local UpdateType needs this for first launch). Until we publish builds, a `dotnet publish` of our content + engine works.
6. Start the service: `sudo systemctl start ss14-watchdog`.
7. Verify:
   - `systemctl is-active ss14-watchdog` → active
   - `pstree -p $(pgrep -f SS14.Watchdog)` → shows `Robust.Server` child
   - `curl -sS http://localhost:5000/instances/vacation-station/ping` responds (exact path per watchdog version)
   - API auth: `curl -u vacation-station:$TOKEN -X POST http://localhost:5000/instances/vacation-station/restart` succeeds
   - Crash recovery: `sudo pkill -f Robust.Server`; confirm watchdog respawns it within TimeoutSeconds (default 60)
8. Firewall:
   - Confirm port 5000 NOT public (`ss -tln | grep :5000` shows 127.0.0.1 only; cloud firewall closed)
   - Confirm UDP 1212 + TCP 1212 open inbound per docs/NETWORKING.md (game server status + main port)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `systemctl is-active ss14-watchdog` → active
- [ ] Game server running as child of watchdog (pstree)
- [ ] ApiToken stored in password manager
- [ ] API ping + auth'd restart both succeed from localhost
- [ ] Crash recovery verified (kill Robust.Server, watchdog respawns)
- [ ] Port 5000 localhost-only; 1212 open per networking doc
- [ ] Record completion + timestamp in this bead before closing

## Notes
- Loki sink in appsettings.yml is intentionally commented out (waits on vs-2p3). Don't enable yet.
- UpdateType remains `Local` until we have a Manifest CDN. Don't change without planning an update pipeline.
- Report any divergence from setup.watchdog.sh so we can PR fixes back.

title: human: verify vs-13x dashboards load in grafana
description:
## Context
vs-13x shipped two adapted Grafana dashboards (game-servers.json, perf-metrics.json) into ops/observability/grafana/dashboards/ plus docs. Grafana's file provider will pick them up automatically on container start, but nobody has actually confirmed they render correctly against live data.

## Dependencies
- vs-25k (observability stack running) complete — Grafana must be up and datasources green

## Task — verify on the host with a running stack
1. Ensure grafana container has the latest dashboards: `docker compose -f ops/observability/docker-compose.yml restart grafana` (the file provider rescans on boot).
2. In Grafana UI → Dashboards: confirm both appear:
   - "Vacation Station Servers" (uid `vs14-game-servers`)
   - "Vacation Station Perf Metrics" (uid `vs14-perf-metrics`)
3. On **Game Servers**:
   - All panels render without the red "Datasource not found" banner
   - Player-count / round-length / server-list panels populate once SS14 is running
   - Connections panel (Postgres) shows at least 1 row (the grafana session itself counts)
4. On **Perf Metrics**:
   - The `$Server` dropdown populates with `vacation-station` from `label_values(ss14_round_length{job="gameservers"}, server)`
   - Logs panel streams live Loki logs: `{App="Robust.Server",Server="vacation-station"}`
   - Perf panels (tick rate, process metrics) populate
5. Known TODOs to either resolve or backlog:
   - **TPS alert notifier** — upstream references notification UID `N5nihcmMk` that doesn't exist locally. Grafana logs "missing notifier"; alert still fires internally but can't deliver. Either create a local contact point + rebind, or delete the alert rule until alerting scope is formally opened.
   - **Stale `byName` overrides** for wizards_den_eu_west / us_west — harmless no-ops on our single-instance deployment; prune at your leisure via UI edit + export.
   - **Datasource uid fragility** — dashboards bind to datasource NAMES (`Prometheus`, `Loki`, `Postgres`). If someone later adds explicit `uid:` fields to datasources.yml, the dashboards will silently unbind. Mitigation: pin datasource uids to lowercase names in datasources.yml AND rewrite the dashboard JSONs to match.
6. If anything breaks, file a follow-up `fix:` bead referencing vs-13x and the specific panel/UID at fault.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Both dashboards visible under Dashboards in Grafana UI with the pinned UIDs
- [ ] All panels on both dashboards render without datasource errors
- [ ] `$Server` variable on Perf Metrics populates from live Prometheus labels
- [ ] Logs panel on Perf Metrics streams live log lines from the running SS14 server
- [ ] Decision recorded on the TPS alert notifier TODO (local contact point or removed)
- [ ] Record completion + timestamp before closing

## Notes
- A Robust.Server must be actively running for most panels to show data. First-boot empty panels are expected; re-check after a round has started.
- Dashboards are provisioned read-only. To iterate, "Save As..." a copy, then export + replace the committed JSON, then `docker compose restart grafana`.

title: human: bring up vs-2p3 observability stack on host
description:
## Context
vs-2p3 shipped docker-compose + prometheus/loki/grafana configs + SS14/watchdog wiring + ops docs. Nothing is running on the host. This bead captures the host-only bring-up.

## Dependencies
- vs-y6o (postgres host setup) complete — Grafana Postgres datasource expects it
- vs-1np (watchdog host setup) complete — Loki receives watchdog + game server logs
- Docker Engine installed on the host (if not yet: `curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sh` or per distro)

## Task — run on the target host
1. **Bootstrap secrets** (NONE committed):
   - `mkdir -p ops/observability/secrets`
   - `openssl rand -base64 32 > ops/observability/secrets/grafana_admin_password.txt`
   - `cp ops/observability/.env.example ops/observability/.env`
   - Edit `ops/observability/.env`: set `POSTGRES_PASSWORD=` to the vs14 Postgres password from password manager
   - Confirm both files gitignored (`.gitignore` in ops/observability/ handles this — verify)
2. **Bring up the stack**:
   - `docker compose -f ops/observability/docker-compose.yml up -d`
   - `docker compose -f ops/observability/docker-compose.yml ps` — all three services healthy
3. **Verify endpoints** (all localhost):
   - `curl -sS localhost:9090/api/v1/targets | jq '.data.activeTargets[] | {job: .labels.job, health}'` — `gameservers` target reports `up` once SS14 is running
   - `curl -sS localhost:3100/ready` → `ready`
   - `curl -sS localhost:3000/api/health` → `{"database":"ok"}`
4. **Grafana login**:
   - Browse to `http://localhost:3000` (port-forward via SSH if remote)
   - Login as `admin` with password from `grafana_admin_password.txt`
   - Change admin password on first login (Grafana forces this)
   - Store the final admin password in password manager under "Vacation Station / grafana / admin"
5. **Datasource verification**:
   - Grafana → Connections → Data sources: Prometheus, Loki, PostgreSQL all auto-provisioned, all test-connection green
   - PostgreSQL datasource only works if `POSTGRES_PASSWORD` in `.env` is correct and Postgres `pg_hba.conf` permits host.docker.internal (127.0.0.1 usually fine)
6. **Metric + log sanity checks** (requires SS14 game server running):
   - In Grafana Explore with Prometheus: `robust_player_count{server="vacation-station"}` returns a series
   - In Grafana Explore with Loki: `{App="Robust.Server",Server="vacation-station"}` returns log lines
   - Watchdog logs: `{Server="vacation-station-watchdog"}` or whatever Name you configured
7. **SS14 side wiring**:
   - Confirm `/opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/config.toml` has the `[metrics]` and `[loki]` blocks from config.toml.example
   - Confirm `/opt/ss14-watchdog/appsettings.yml` has the `GrafanaLoki` Serilog sink enabled
   - Restart watchdog to pick up changes: `sudo systemctl restart ss14-watchdog`
8. **Firewall**:
   - `ss -tln | grep -E ':(9090|3100|3000)'` — all three bound to 127.0.0.1 only
   - Public Grafana via Caddy only (see docs/NETWORKING.md snippet)
9. **Caddy** (if external access desired):
   - Add the `grafana.yourdomain.com` block from docs/NETWORKING.md to your Caddyfile
   - `sudo systemctl reload caddy`
   - Verify HTTPS cert issued and proxy works

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docker compose ps` shows prometheus + loki + grafana healthy
- [ ] `curl localhost:9090/api/v1/targets` shows `gameservers` target `up`
- [ ] `curl localhost:3100/ready` → `ready`
- [ ] Grafana admin password rotated on first login and stored in password manager
- [ ] All three datasources test-green in Grafana UI
- [ ] Prometheus + Loki sample queries return data (once SS14 is running)
- [ ] Ports 9090/3100/3000 localhost-only (confirmed with ss)
- [ ] Caddy HTTPS proxy functional (if external Grafana desired)
- [ ] Record completion timestamp before closing

## Notes
- Dashboards (vs-13x) land separately — this bead is stack bring-up only.
- If Prometheus `gameservers` target shows `down`, check that SS14 `[metrics] host = "localhost"` and the watchdog instance has actually started the game server on port 44880.
- Loki retention is 30d — raise by editing `ops/observability/loki-config.yml` `retention_period` and restarting the loki container.

title: human: run setup.ubuntu.sh --server on prod host
description:
## Context
All the other prod human beads (vs-y6o postgres, vs-1np watchdog, vs-25k observability, vs-205 dashboards) assume the host already has the baseline runtime: .NET 10 SDK + ASP.NET Core 10 runtime, git, python, build tools, firewall tooling. `setup.ubuntu.sh` is the committed script that does that. This bead is the bootstrap step they all depend on.

Nix is the primary DEV path (see docs/DEVELOPMENT.md and /nix skill), but production hosts run system-installed .NET via this script — not nix.

## Dependencies
- None (this is the root of the prod install tree)

## Blocks
- vs-y6o (postgres host setup)
- vs-1np (watchdog host setup) — specifically needs .NET 10 SDK to `dotnet publish` SS14.Watchdog from source
- vs-25k (observability stack bring-up) — docker is typically installed separately but `setup.ubuntu.sh --server` may or may not include it (verify)
- vs-205 (dashboard verification) — transitively via vs-25k

## Task — run on the target host

1. **Fresh-host sanity**: `lsb_release -a` should report Ubuntu 22.04 or 24.04 (what setup.ubuntu.sh targets). If not, confirm parity or switch host.
2. **Clone repo**: `git clone https://github.com/azigler/vacation-station-14.git /opt/vacation-station` (or wherever you want production repo root; this matches the path ops/watchdog/ss14-backup.service assumes).
3. **Run setup.ubuntu.sh**: `cd /opt/vacation-station && sudo ./setup.ubuntu.sh --server`.
   - `--server` flag omits the client-only graphics/audio deps (no X, no OpenGL, no PulseAudio). Keeps the host minimal.
   - If `--server` flag isn't implemented yet in the committed script, check what flags it takes and pick the server-minimal path; if only a single mode exists, run without flag and report what got installed.
   - Script should install: .NET 10 SDK, ASP.NET Core 10 runtime, git, python3, curl, build-essential.
4. **Verify .NET**: `dotnet --version` reports a 10.x SDK; `dotnet --list-runtimes` includes `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App 10.x`.
5. **Verify python**: `python3 --version` ≥ 3.7 (for `RUN_THIS.py`).
6. **Initialize submodule**: `cd /opt/vacation-station && python3 RUN_THIS.py` — pulls RobustToolbox.
7. **Install Docker** (if not already, needed for vs-25k observability stack): `curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sudo sh` and `sudo usermod -aG docker $USER` (log out/in or `newgrp docker`). Verify: `docker compose version` → v2.x.
8. **Firewall baseline** (see docs/NETWORKING.md for full detail): `sudo ufw allow OpenSSH`, `sudo ufw enable`. Other per-service ports come from subsequent beads (1212 for SS14, etc.).
9. **Create ops user** (if setup.ubuntu.sh doesn't): `ss14` system user + group referenced by the watchdog systemd unit (vs-h3u script creates it but sanity-check here that `id ss14` works after its install).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Ubuntu 22.04 or 24.04 confirmed
- [ ] `/opt/vacation-station` (or chosen prod path) cloned, main up-to-date
- [ ] `setup.ubuntu.sh` ran cleanly (no error exit)
- [ ] `dotnet --version` ≥ 10.0.x
- [ ] `dotnet --list-runtimes` includes `Microsoft.AspNetCore.App 10.x`
- [ ] `python3 RUN_THIS.py` completes; `RobustToolbox/` is populated
- [ ] `docker compose version` returns ≥ v2.x (install separately if setup.ubuntu.sh doesn't cover it)
- [ ] `ufw status` shows enabled with at least OpenSSH allowed
- [ ] Record completion timestamp + any setup.ubuntu.sh deviations in this bead before closing

## Notes
- setup.ubuntu.sh is *checked in* from upstream Delta-V; it may get updates via upstream-sync. Always pull before running.
- This bead is the **system-install bootstrap**. Contributors doing dev work should use nix/direnv instead (see docs/DEVELOPMENT.md Quick Start (Nix)).
- If anything in `setup.ubuntu.sh` fails or drifts from reality, file a `fix:` bead so we can PR corrections back (especially if deps change in future .NET / Ubuntu releases).
- This bead captures only baseline OS-level setup. Service-specific bring-up (postgres, watchdog, observability, dashboards) are in vs-y6o, vs-1np, vs-25k, vs-205 respectively — run them in that order after this one.

title: ops: bootstrap vacation-station-14 website (monorepo at web/)
description:
## Context
VS14 needs a public web presence: landing page explaining the server,
connect instructions, rules, live status, blog/news, credits. Ideally
served from the same host (Caddy) that fronts Grafana and the watchdog,
same TLS cert, same ops pipeline.

Host `vacation.yourdomain.com` (or whatever top-level we pick) as the
player-facing front door. SS14 has no convention-standard server site —
every community runs their own. Examples: deltavstation.com,
wizardsdenaz.io, tg-station-13.org.

## Monorepo placement
```
vacation-station-14/
├── Content.Server/ ...   (existing)
├── web/                  (NEW: this bead)
│   ├── app/              (Next.js 15 app router OR whatever flavor)
│   ├── components/
│   ├── content/          markdown source for rules, about, etc.
│   ├── public/           static assets
│   ├── package.json
│   ├── bun.lock
│   ├── next.config.mjs
│   └── README.md
├── services/ ...
└── ops/ ...
```

`web/` lives alongside the game code. Separate build pipeline (bun / next
build) from the dotnet build. CI adds a web-lint/build job. Deploys to the
same host as the game via systemd + Caddy.

## Pages (minimum viable)

### Public
1. **/** — Landing. Hero: "Vacation Station 14 — hard fork of Delta-V"
   with server status widget (live player count / map / round id pulled
   from `http://51.81.33.136:1212/info` via API route with 15s cache).
   Download launcher button. ss14:// connect button.
2. **/rules** — The rules (rendered from `docs/community/rules.md`, SSOT
   with Discord bot + game MOTD)
3. **/about** — What VS14 is, fork lineage (upstream SS14 → Delta-V → VS14),
   contributors, license (AGPL-3.0 + MIT)
4. **/connect** — Launcher download links, step-by-step first-time-player
   instructions, troubleshooting (auth required, firewall, etc.)
5. **/credits** — Auto-generated from contributors (see vs-39z)
6. **/blog** or **/news** — Tagged markdown posts for announcements,
   patch notes, event recaps. Low-maintenance: write a markdown file,
   push, it ships.

### Player-facing tools (if we fork the ancillary services per vs-19h)
7. **/cookbook** — Crafting recipe browser (if we fork the SS14 cookbook)
8. **/maps** — Map thumbnails + stats (from Content.MapRenderer output)
9. **/docs** — Player-facing docs (if we fork the SS14 docs site into here)

### Admin-facing
10. **/admin** — Auth-gated panel fronted by Wizden OAuth or GitHub. Shows:
    - Live server stats (proxies to internal Grafana)
    - Recent rounds + replays (vs-2l1)
    - Ban list (reads from postgres)
    - Announcement composer (hits watchdog /announce endpoint)

## Tech stack — proposed
- **Next.js 15** (React Server Components + app router) — matches what
  you've already got running (`next-server` seen in `top`). Fastest
  time-to-ship for a SEO-friendly site with live data.
- **bun** as runtime + package manager — matches slack-bot / future
  discord-bot.
- **Tailwind CSS** — no-decision default for rapid theming.
- **shadcn/ui** or similar — component primitives.
- **MDX** for content pages — markdown + React components.
- **No database** on the web side for Phase 1. Reads from: SS14 `/info`
  HTTP, Grafana API (for metrics), existing postgres (read-only for
  admin panel later).

## Integration points
- **Live server status**: API route `/api/server-status` calls
  `http://127.0.0.1:1212/info`, caches 15s, returns JSON. Homepage
  widget consumes it.
- **Bead → blog** auto-post (optional, later): close a bead with a
  `type:announcement` label → post appears under /news.
- **Discord webhook mirror**: new blog post triggers a Discord
  `#announcements` post (bot does the translation).
- **Content sources of truth**: rules.md, motd.md, positioning.md live
  under `docs/community/` in the repo root. Web reads them via FS at
  build time. Discord bot reads them at runtime. Game server reads them
  at restart (via config.toml replacement during deploy).

## Deployment
- systemd unit: `vs14-web.service` — `ExecStart=bun run start` in
  `/home/ubuntu/vacation-station-14/web`
- Port: 3300 (sequential with grafana 3200, staying above the
  3000-collision zone)
- Caddy: `vacation.yourdomain.com` → `127.0.0.1:3300` with automatic HTTPS
- Build step: Content and rebuild on every `git pull` (or CI builds
  ahead of deploy)

## Phased rollout

**Phase 1**: Minimum viable
- Scaffold web/ with Next.js + tailwind
- Home + rules + about + connect + credits pages
- Server-status widget on home
- Caddy routing, systemd unit, deploy script

**Phase 2**: Content pipeline
- MDX blog / news
- Single-source-of-truth integration with Discord bot + game MOTD
  (rules.md / motd.md SSOT)
- Auto-generated credits from git + Patreon (see vs-39z)

**Phase 3**: Integrations
- Embed / link ancillary services (cookbook, maps) per vs-19h outcomes
- Admin panel (gated)

**Phase 4**: Community polish
- SEO metadata + OG tags
- Analytics (self-hosted Plausible or Umami if you care)
- RSS feed for /news

## Dependencies
- **vs-3e3** (content writing) — can ship Phase 1 with placeholder copy
  BUT real launch needs real copy
- **vs-2y8** (Caddy+HTTPS stack active) — web needs Caddy fronting
- **vs-19h** (ancillary services assessment) — determines Phase 3 scope

## Acceptance Criteria (Phase 1)
- [ ] `web/` exists in repo with a Next.js 15 scaffold
- [ ] Home / rules / about / connect / credits pages render locally
- [ ] `/api/server-status` returns live data from SS14 `/info`
- [ ] `vs14-web.service` systemd unit runs via bun
- [ ] Caddyfile block for `vacation.yourdomain.com → :3300` with HTTPS
- [ ] First HTTP 200 served from public domain
- [ ] Lighthouse score reasonable (mobile + desktop ≥80 perf)

## Out of scope for Phase 1
- User accounts / login (Wizden OAuth is Phase 3 admin panel)
- Payments / donations
- In-browser game client (that's SS14's own deal, separate project)
- Realtime presence (needs websocket; later)

## Open questions
- Which domain? You said "yourdomain.com" — pick one before Caddy work.
- Monorepo build tool: pure Next.js or Turborepo/Nx if we go big? Default:
  plain Next.js for Phase 1; adopt Turborepo only if `web/` + `services/discord-bot/`
  start sharing code meaningfully.
- Styling: Tailwind (fast) vs vanilla CSS modules. Default Tailwind.
- Font / brand: needs a design pass. Placeholder: system fonts + a color
  palette chosen quickly.

title: fix: silence grafana paths.plugins dir-missing log noise
description:
## Context
After vs-2yf fixed the grafana preinstall plugin errors, two non-fatal log lines remain when booting `nix run .#dev-services`:
```
Failed to get renderer plugin sources
Failed to load external plugins
```
These originate from grafana's `paths.plugins` setting pointing at a non-existent directory when `declarativePlugins` is null. Non-blocking — grafana boots and serves fine — just log noise.

## Task
Silence the two log lines in the dev services-flake grafana config.

Approach options (pick whichever the services-flake grafana module actually supports):
- **A**: set `services.grafana.graf1.extraConf.paths.plugins` to a writable dev-local path (e.g. `./.data/grafana/plugins`) so grafana finds an empty-but-present dir
- **B**: set `services.grafana.graf1.declarativePlugins = [ ]` explicitly instead of null so the module builds a proper empty plugins dir in the nix store
- **C**: override the grafana.ini `paths.plugins` to an empty string or `/dev/null`-ish path that suppresses the scan (verify with grafana 12.3 behavior)

Whatever path you take, verify with the smoke test below.

## Validation
Prepend `eval "$(direnv export bash)" 2>/dev/null;` to nix commands.

```
rm -rf .data
nix run .#dev-services -- --tui=false > /tmp/vs14-stack.log 2>&1 &
sleep 60
grep -E "Failed to (get renderer|load external) plugin" /tmp/vs14-stack.log | wc -l   # expect 0
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "grafana: %{http_code}\n" http://localhost:3000/api/health    # expect 200
pkill -f process-compose; rm -rf .data
```

Also confirm the other three services still work (prom/loki/pg) — don't regress vs-2yf.

## Out of scope
- Don't touch any service except grafana
- Don't modify prod observability
- Don't add real plugins to the dev stack

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `grep` of the two specific "Failed to ..." lines returns 0 matches after 60s boot
- [ ] Grafana health still 200
- [ ] Prometheus, Loki, Postgres still healthy (vs-2yf validation unchanged)
- [ ] Single commit, `:bug:` gitmoji, `Bead: vs-2kv` trailer

## Notes
If none of the approaches silence the warnings cleanly, document what you tried and close this as "acceptable log noise" with a commented note in flake.nix. The warnings are harmless — do not bend the flake into a pretzel over them.

title: ops: enable replay auto-record + retention + training-data pipeline
description:
## Context
SS14 ships per-round replay recording: each round can be captured as a zip
containing per-tick state snapshots + checkpoints + metadata (map, gamemode,
players, round id, engine version). Playback is deterministic on the same
engine version.

**Current state** (VS14 day-1):
- `replay.server_recording_enabled = true` (engine default; recording
  machinery is loaded)
- `replay.auto_record = false` (content default; no per-round auto-start)
- Default location `<instance>/data/replays/<template>.zip`
- No retention policy, no extraction pipeline, no cold storage

User goal: **collect every round as training data for an eventual ML
pipeline.** Treat replays as research assets, not transient debug artifacts.

## Scope

### Phase 1 — enable auto-record with safe defaults (committable today)
1. Add `[replay]` block to `instances/vacation-station/config.toml.example`:
   ```toml
   [replay]
   auto_record = true
   # Nested path for sensible ls / sharding / retention globbing
   auto_record_name = "{year}/{month}/round_{round}-{day}T{hour}{minute}Z.zip"
   # Optional staging dir — on fast local disk, moved to final location
   # only after round ends. Useful if final dir is on a slow NFS mount.
   # auto_record_temp_dir = "/var/tmp/vs14-replays-staging"
   record_admin_chat = false  # explicit re-assert upstream default; keeps
                              # admin channel out of training data
   ```
   Apply the same block to the LIVE `/opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/config.toml`.

2. Document caps (inherited from Robust defaults — only override with
   intent):
   - `replay.max_compressed_size = 512MB`
   - `replay.max_uncompressed_size = 1GB`
   - Recording auto-stops when either cap hits — partial replay is kept.

3. Disk budget math: at 1–3 GB/day (8–12 rounds) on the current 174 GB free
   partition, unrotated replays fill disk in 2–6 months. Retention pipeline
   (Phase 2) prevents that.

### Phase 2 — retention + rotation (local)
Mirror the pg_dump pattern in `ops/postgres/backup.sh`:

4. **`ops/replays/rotate.sh`** — keep last N days on local disk, compress
   older metadata-only sidecars, delete raw zips past retention.
   - Daily keep: 14 days of raw zips
   - Metadata-only keep: 180 days (extract `replay_final.yml` + round stats
     before deleting the zip; the yml is tiny, lets us index what we had)
   - Configurable via env vars so it generalizes.

5. **`ops/replays/ss14-replay-rotate.service` + `.timer`** — daily systemd
   timer (like `ss14-backup`), runs as `ss14` user, ReadWritePaths scoped.

6. Update `docs/OPERATIONS.md` Replays section.

### Phase 3 — cold-storage archive (S3-compatible)
7. **`ops/replays/archive.sh`** — before local rotate deletes, upload raw
   zips to S3/Backblaze B2/Hetzner Object Storage. Structure:
   ```
   s3://vs14-replays/
     year=2026/month=04/day=12/round_123-...zip
     year=2026/month=04/day=12/round_123-...metadata.json
   ```
   Partitioned so ML training can filter by date / map / gamemode cheaply.

8. Credentials: S3 access keys via
   `/etc/vacation-station/replay-archive.env` (not in repo, not in git),
   loaded by systemd `EnvironmentFile=`.

9. Upload verification: after successful upload, write a `.uploaded`
   marker; rotate only deletes local zips that have a marker. Prevents
   silent data loss on transient S3 failures.

### Phase 4 — training-data catalog
10. **`ops/replays/index.py`** (or Rust, matching repo conventions) — walk
    the local + S3 replay tree, parse each replay's `replay_final.yml`,
    emit a single `index.parquet` / `index.jsonl` with columns:
    - `round_id`, `timestamp_utc`, `map`, `gamemode`, `player_count`,
      `round_duration_s`, `engine_version`, `content_hash`, `s3_key`,
      `file_size_bytes`
11. Rebuild index on each replay completion + nightly.
12. Downstream consumers (not built here) can query this to pick training
    subsets (e.g. "all SecretSanta rounds on saltern with >30 players from
    2026-Q2").

### Phase 5 — PII + content policy (policy doc, not code)
Before any external distribution of replays:
13. Replays bundle the chat stream EXCEPT admin chat (we already set
    `record_admin_chat = false`).
14. Regular player chat still includes Wizards Den usernames. Some
    jurisdictions treat usernames as PII.
15. **Decide**: private-only training (no publish) needs no scrubbing.
    Public-publish training needs a chat-scrub pass before upload OR
    publicly-usable-variant of each replay.
16. Document the chosen policy in `docs/OPERATIONS.md` and, if
    public-publish, build a `scripts/scrub_replay.py` that rewrites chat
    frames and emits a `scrubbed` variant.

### Phase 6 — engine version pinning for playback
17. Replay format is tied to a specific Robust engine version + content
    assembly hash. `replay_final.yml` records these; training tooling
    MUST pin to a matching runtime. Document this in the catalog schema.
18. When we bump engine via upstream-sync, add a test that plays back at
    least one recent replay to confirm format compatibility — catches
    breaking engine changes before they strand an archive.

## Dependencies
- **vs-3ty done** (postgres running) — no direct link, but shares the
  timer+service pattern.
- **vs-25k done** (obs stack) — Phase 2 rotation should export a
  Prometheus metric for zips archived / zips pruned / backlog depth so
  Grafana can alert on runaway replay growth.

## Acceptance Criteria

**Phase 1 (must-have, blocks landing):**
- [ ] `[replay]` block in `config.toml.example` with `auto_record = true`
- [ ] Live config.toml patched; game server restarted; verified a round
      produced a zip at
      `/opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/data/replays/…`
- [ ] `docs/OPERATIONS.md` has a Replays section covering the defaults,
      where they land, and how to manually trigger via admin commands
- [ ] Disk-growth monitoring added (at minimum: prometheus query or
      doc-level watch item)

**Phase 2 (follow-up, same bead):**
- [ ] `ops/replays/rotate.sh` + systemd timer running daily
- [ ] 14d local raw / 180d metadata retention verified on real data
- [ ] Prometheus metric for replay-dir size

**Phase 3 (follow-up, may split to separate bead):**
- [ ] S3 upload script with credential loading from env file
- [ ] `.uploaded` marker protocol preventing pre-upload deletes

**Phases 4–6**: tracked separately (future beads) once Phase 1–3 land.

## Out of scope for this bead
- The ML training code itself — this bead is about CAPTURING training
  data correctly and durably. Model training is a separate project
- Replay viewer UX improvements
- Changing Robust's replay format or bumping caps

## Notes
- Admin manual commands exist (`replaystart`, `replaystop`) for ad-hoc
  captures outside the auto-record flow.
- The `auto_record_name` template supports subdir splits — the
  `{year}/{month}/` partition scheme above makes s3 rotation globs and
  ML-training subset queries dramatically easier than a flat dir.
- Format stability is the real long-term risk. Pin engine versions in the
  catalog and don't delete replays whose engine version is no longer
  supported without archiving them first.
- When VS14 diverges far from upstream Robust, we may need to take over
  replay format maintenance ourselves. Not today, but be aware.

title: ops: bootstrap discord server + watchdog integration + community bot
description:
## Context
A SS14 community server needs a Discord. Discord is where:
- Rules are posted + acknowledged
- Players ask admins for help when they're not in-game
- Crash alerts + restart notifications flow from the watchdog
- Round announcements happen (end summaries, next-round pings)
- Admin coordination happens (admin-only channels)
- Moderation actions are discussed + recorded
- Bans are appealed
- Community events are planned

Discord also acts as an identity / verification backbone — many SS14
communities tie in-game admin status to Discord roles.

## Scope — phased rollout

### Phase 1: create the server + minimum viable channels
1. Create a new Discord server "Vacation Station 14" (owner: azigler).
2. Enable **Community** mode (unlocks announcement channels, screening).
3. Channel layout (start conservative; expand later):
   - `#welcome` — auto-sends new joiners the rules + connect info
   - `#announcements` — admin-only posts, everyone reads
   - `#rules` — formal ruleset (see vs-3e3); read-only for members
   - `#general` — main chat
   - `#off-topic` — low-stakes chat
   - `#bug-reports` — structured ticket intake (thread-per-report)
   - `#suggestions` — feature / content requests, upvotes via reactions
   - `#server-status` — webhook target for watchdog crash alerts + status
     transitions (private to @Admin; public mirror optional)
   - `#admin-only` — staff coordination
   - `#admin-logs` — every admin action (ban/kick/mute) mirrored here
     from the game server
   - `#appeals` — ban-appeal intake (private threads)
4. Roles (minimum):
   - `@Admin` — game-server admin powers; can moderate Discord too
   - `@Moderator` — Discord-only moderation (optional, smaller role)
   - `@Verified` — confirmed via Wizards Den OAuth (optional for now)
   - `@Everyone` — default
   - Pingable role `@Players` for opt-in announcement pings

### Phase 2: watchdog + game-server hooks
1. Create a Discord incoming webhook in `#server-status`.
2. Populate `/opt/ss14-watchdog/appsettings.yml` `Notification.DiscordWebhook`
   with the URL. Restart watchdog. Crash + restart events now DM the channel.
3. Create a second webhook in `#admin-logs`.
4. Configure SS14 admin-log Discord export (cvar: `discord.logs_webhook`
   or similar — verify exact cvar name in CVars.cs). Every kick/ban posts.
5. Create a third webhook in `#announcements` for round-end summaries
   (uses SS14's round-end discord integration).

### Phase 3: community bot (ours)
A dedicated bot living in the monorepo at `services/discord-bot/` that
provides:
- `/status` slash command — returns live server status from
  `http://51.81.33.136:1212/info` (player count, round id, map, auth mode)
- `/rules` slash command — posts the current rules from a markdown file
  in-repo (single source of truth for website + Discord)
- `/connect` — returns the ss14:// URL + download link for the launcher
- Round start / end / player-count threshold announcements via watchdog
  webhook parsing or direct SS14 status polling
- Ban appeal workflow (private threads auto-created when @Admin reacts
  to a ban-log message with 🛡️)
- Bead webhook — POST to a Discord channel when a new P1 bead is filed
  (uses beads CLI's webhook feature if it has one, else a tiny poller)
- Optional: chat bridge (in-game OOC ↔ Discord `#general`) — complex,
  usually gated behind auth level

Tech: likely **bun + discord.js** since the host is already running bun
for slack-bot. Or Python + discord.py if we prefer. Either way, lives in
the monorepo and shares ops patterns with other services.

### Phase 4: community automations
1. **Autorole**: give new joiners `@Everyone` + screening gate
2. **Automod**: built-in Discord automod for spam / slurs
3. **Reaction roles**: opt-in for pingable roles (e.g. `@Players` for
   round-start pings, `@EventNotify` for community events)
4. **Welcome DM**: auto-DM new joiners a rules-digest + connect info
5. **Verification via Wizards Den OAuth**: when a player authenticates
   to our game server via Wizden, mark them `@Verified` in Discord.
   Requires the community bot + game-side hook.
6. **Ticket system**: Tickets for ban appeals, complex bug reports,
   admin concerns. Built-in thread-based OR use a Discord add-on like
   Ticket Tool.

### Phase 5: integration polish
1. **Single source of truth**: rules / MOTD / positioning in
   `docs/community/rules.md`, `docs/community/motd.md`. Bot + website +
   game server all read the same files at build / deploy time.
2. **Discord → game chat bridge** (optional, opinionated).
3. **Donations / Patreon**: Discord role sync if we stand up a donation
   platform. Not urgent.
4. **Admin panel**: private admin channel has a bot command to issue
   server-wide announcements (`/announce "Round starting in 5 minutes"`
   → hits the watchdog API → game MOTD banner).

## Repo layout proposal
```
services/discord-bot/
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts          entry point (bun run)
│   ├── commands/         slash command handlers
│   ├── events/           discord gateway event handlers
│   ├── integrations/
│   │   ├── ss14-status.ts     polls game server /info
│   │   ├── watchdog.ts        parses watchdog webhook events
│   │   └── admin-logs.ts      parses SS14 admin log stream
│   └── utils/
├── package.json
├── bun.lock
├── README.md
├── systemd/
│   ├── install.sh
│   └── vs14-discord-bot.service
└── .env.example         (real .env gitignored)
```

systemd unit follows the `slack-bot.service` pattern: user unit,
`Type=notify` if discord.js supports it, watchdog timer, journald
output, `EnvironmentFile=/home/ubuntu/vacation-station-14/services/discord-bot/.env`.

## Dependencies
- **vs-2y8** (watchdog behind Caddy+HTTPS) — webhook URLs should be
  HTTPS once Caddy's up
- **vs-3e3** (content writing) — bot can't post rules until rules are
  written. Phases 1-2 don't block on content; Phase 3+ do.
- **vs-2dr** (website) — single-source-of-truth content in-repo implies
  the website exists to consume the same content

## Acceptance Criteria

**Phase 1 (standalone deliverable):**
- [ ] Discord server exists with the channel layout above
- [ ] Roles created, basic permissions set
- [ ] Community mode enabled

**Phase 2:**
- [ ] Watchdog crash-alert webhook posts to `#server-status`
- [ ] Kill a test round, confirm the post
- [ ] Admin-log webhook streaming kicks/bans to `#admin-logs`

**Phase 3:**
- [ ] `services/discord-bot/` exists in repo, builds, runs via systemd
- [ ] `/status`, `/rules`, `/connect` slash commands work
- [ ] Bot posts round start / end in `#announcements`

**Phase 4:**
- [ ] Reaction roles wired
- [ ] Welcome flow tested with a throwaway account
- [ ] Wizden OAuth verification working OR documented as "later"

**Phase 5:**
- [ ] Rules/MOTD are single-source-of-truth (one file → 3 consumers)
- [ ] `/announce` slash command wired to game MOTD

## Out of scope
- Bridging voice chat / in-game voice (not a thing SS14 supports)
- Full social network features (profiles, friend lists, etc. — use
  Discord's built-ins)
- Replacing Discord with a self-hosted chat (Matrix, etc.)

## Open questions
- Which Discord bot library (discord.js vs discord.py)? Default: discord.js
  for parity with slack-bot's bun runtime.
- Do we want the chat bridge (OOC ↔ Discord)? Worth-it only once we have
  active users.
- Verification via Wizden OAuth requires hooking into the launcher's auth
  flow — feasible but non-trivial. Punt to Phase 4 or later.

title: ops: prometheus + loki + grafana stack
description:
## Context
Level 5 observability stack: Prometheus for metrics, Loki for logs, Grafana for
visualization. SS14 server has native support for both (port 44880 exposes
Prometheus metrics, Serilog.Sinks.Loki pushes logs directly).

SS14.Watchdog also supports Loki push via its own Serilog config.

## Dependencies
- vs-h3u (watchdog) should be in progress or done (watchdog-level metrics/logs are valuable)
- vs-3ty (postgres) done (Grafana Connections panel queries Postgres)

## Task
Deploy Prometheus + Loki + Grafana via docker-compose on the same host as the
game server. Wire SS14 server and Watchdog to push metrics and logs.

## Acceptance Criteria

### docker-compose stack
- [ ] ops/docker-compose.yml in repo (or ops/observability/docker-compose.yml)
- [ ] Services: prometheus, loki, grafana (consider alertmanager later)
- [ ] Persistent volumes for each (survive container restart)
- [ ] Network isolation (stack internal, only Grafana exposed via Caddy)

### Prometheus
- [ ] ops/prometheus.yml scrape config:
  - job_name: "gameservers" (MUST match dashboard expectations)
  - Static target: host.docker.internal:44880 (or bridge network)
  - Labels: server: "vacation-station" (matches Loki name)
  - scrape_interval: 15s (docs say 1s, that's too aggressive)
- [ ] Prometheus UI reachable at localhost:9090
- [ ] Targets showing "UP" status
- [ ] Sample query `robust_player_count` returns data

### Loki
- [ ] ops/loki-config.yml with retention policy
- [ ] Loki reachable at localhost:3100
- [ ] Labels filter works: `{App="Robust.Server",Server="vacation-station"}`
- [ ] Retention configured (e.g. 30 days)

### SS14 server config
- [ ] instances/vacation-station/config.toml:
  [metrics]
  enabled = true
  host = "localhost"
  port = 44880
  [loki]
  enabled = true
  address = "http://localhost:3100"
  name = "vacation-station"   # MUST match Prometheus `server` label

### SS14.Watchdog config
- [ ] /opt/ss14-watchdog/appsettings.yml Serilog Loki sink:
  - Address: http://localhost:3100
  - Name: vacation-station-watchdog (or similar)

### Grafana
- [ ] Reachable at localhost:3000 (fronted by Caddy for HTTPS)
- [ ] Admin password from password manager (not default)
- [ ] Datasources configured:
  - Prometheus: http://prometheus:9090
  - Loki: http://loki:3100
  - PostgreSQL (grafana-postgresql-datasource plugin): Vacation Station DB
- [ ] grafana-postgresql-datasource plugin installed

### Firewall
- [ ] Prometheus, Loki internal-only (not on public ports)
- [ ] Grafana behind Caddy with HTTPS (docs/NETWORKING.md pattern)
- [ ] Caddy reverse proxy: grafana.yourdomain.com -> localhost:3000

### Documentation
- [ ] docs/OPERATIONS.md observability section
- [ ] Troubleshooting: no metrics showing, no logs showing
- [ ] Instructions for adding alerts later
